response:
Sec. 1  
peanuts are a type of food that contains proteins which can trigger allergic reactions in some individuals  
allergic reactions can cause severe symptoms such as difficulty breathing and swelling of the lips  
these reactions can be life-threatening and require immediate medical attention  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot consume them and must take precautions to avoid exposure  
underwater environments do not contain peanuts and are not a source of allergens  
the lack of peanuts in underwater environments means that allergic reactions are not a concern there  

Sec. 2  
the human body is designed to handle allergens from food and not from other sources  
underwater environments do not have the same allergens as land-based environments  
the absence of peanuts in underwater settings means that allergic reactions are not a factor  
people who are allergic to peanuts can live underwater as long as they avoid exposure to peanuts  
the environment underwater does not pose a risk to those with peanut allergies
